capability_objective: The registrar is reconciling two course entries for the enrollment desk. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the course named “Nonprofit Budgeting Workshop” at “Civic Center” and the course named “Oral History Methods” at “Library Annex”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use a two-row table with columns Name, ID, Date, and Status; render any absent field as “unknown” and do not infer it.
prompt:
The registrar needs a reconciled course-entry audit for the enrollment desk. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `course-audit.md` from full course records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `COURSES.md`, then use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./coursectl` executable for every course-registry lookup; use its built-in help to discover the interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the course named “Nonprofit Budgeting Workshop” at “Civic Center” and the course named “Oral History Methods” at “Library Annex”. Your first course-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./coursectl` processes, with no other course operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following course-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./coursectl` processes. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take IDs, dates, and statuses for resolved branches only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter course data. Do not expose locations, coordinators, notes, unrelated matches, or other full-record fields in the deliverable.

`course-audit.md` must contain exactly one Markdown table followed immediately by one comparison line, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other content. The table must have exactly these four columns in this order: `Name`, `ID`, `Date`, and `Status`; its two data rows must represent the requested courses in the requested order. Use the requested course name when a branch is unresolved. For every absent ID, date, or status, use the literal lowercase word `unknown`; never infer a value. Preserve every retrieved value exactly. If both retrieved statuses are present and identical, the final line must be `Status comparison: both are <status>.`; if both are present and differ, it must be `Status comparison: <first status> differs from <second status>.`; otherwise it must be exactly `Status comparison: unknown.` Use standard Markdown table delimiters with one space around each cell value and `---` in every separator cell. End every line with a newline. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied and protected files unchanged. Return only the table and comparison line in your final response.
